headline: South Florida economy cooling but staying strong—tech, tourism, and housing showing mixed signals

stories.title: Tech Hub Pulse Shows South Florida's Next Chapter: From Capital Attraction to Deployment
stories.summary: South Florida just hosted Tech Hub Pulse where hundreds of leaders gathered to discuss the region's shift from attracting capital to actually deploying it through 2026-2030. Speakers predicted a surge in space/defense tech investment and potentially 3-5 IPOs within 60 months as the region positions itself as a serious tech player.
stories.takeaway: South Florida's transitioning fr just being a place where money comes to being a place where it actually gets deployed—that's literally the next growth cycle.

stories.title: D-Wave Relocates Quantum Computing HQ to Boca Raton—FAU Buys $20M Quantum Computer
stories.summary: The world's leading quantum computing company just moved from California to Boca Raton, and Florida Atlantic University grabbed a $20M quantum computer from them. This is lowkey huge for South Florida's tech credentials and positions the region as a quantum computing hub.
stories.takeaway: Quantum computing is officially moving to South Florida—this signals real tech heavyweight interest beyond just crypto and finance bros.

stories.title: Florida's Housing Market Cooling: Prices Stabilizing as Inventory Rises
stories.summary: After years of insane price growth, South Florida's real estate market is finally cooling down—inventory is coming back and prices are stabilizing. The Florida Chamber Foundation says this normalization is actually healthy and sets buyers up better for 2026.
stories.takeaway: Housing prices aren't exploding anymore, which honestly might be good news for people who actually want to buy instead of just speculate.

stories.title: Consumer Confidence Rising in Florida—Buyers Getting More Optimistic
stories.summary: Consumer sentiment in Florida just climbed for the third straight month, reflecting stronger financial outlooks that shape major buying decisions. People are fr starting to feel better about their wallets even as the broader economy moderates.
stories.takeaway: Despite economic slowdown vibes, people actually feel more confident about their finances—that's the floor holding up tbh.

watchNext.title: Fort Lauderdale's Economic Impact Growth Reaches 20% Since 2022
watchNext.why: Downtown Fort Lauderdale is quietly becoming a serious economic engine with tech, finance and entertainment—good context for understanding the tri-county power shift.

watchNext.title: Tourism Outlook: South Florida Expects 1-3% Growth in 2026 Despite Airport Slowdown
watchNext.why: Visit Lauderdale's new campaign and hotel openings suggest tourism can recover even as airport traffic dipped—worth watching how events like Formula 1 move the needle.
